title: soliva anthemifolia var. acaulis
intro: Soliva anthemifolia var. acaulis, also known as Wild Chamomile or Pineapple Weed, is an annual herb that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil content. The health benefits of Soliva anthemifolia var. acaulis include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
